records.Description: Under the National Critical Minerals Mission (NCMM), Ministry of Mines has allocated INR 1,500 crores to incentivize the establishment of recycling capacity in the country. The Scheme will have a tenure of six years from FY 2025-26 to FY 2030-31 and provide both Capex and Opex incentives to industry.
records.Completed deliverables:

2. DPR & Eligibility Document submitted.

2. Technical bid to be submitted
records.Description:
A first-of-its-kind initiative with a total financial outlay of ₹7,280 crore, aimed at establishing 6,000 MTPA of integrated Rare Earth Permanent Magnet (REPM) manufacturing capacity in India.
The scheme comprises a capital subsidy of ₹750 crore for setting up integrated facilities and sales-linked incentives of ₹6,450 crore disbursed over five years, with up to five beneficiaries each allocated 600–1,200 MTPA of capacity through a global competitive bidding process.

records.Scheme: MEITY Electronics Component Manufacturing Scheme (ECMS) - REPM Project
records.Description: Notified in April 2025 with a ₹40,000 crore budget outlay over six years, ECMS offers turnover-linked, capex-linked and hybrid incentives to boost domestic electronics component production. Incentives are disbursed across sub-assemblies, bare components, selected bare components, and supply-chain/capital equipment categories to attract investment and reduce import dependence.

The Research, Development and Innovation Fund (RDIF) is a flagship DST initiative with a total outlay of ₹1 lakh crore over 6 years, aimed at catalysing private sector investment in R&D and innovation. 

The Technology Development Board (TDB) acts as a Second Level Fund Manager (SLFM) under the scheme, disbursing funds to eligible Indian-registered entities working on technologies at TRL 4+. Funding is structured as long-tenure, collateral-free loans at 3–4% interest over 15 years, covering up to 50% of assessed project cost.
records.Completed deliverables: 1. Comprehensive project list from PnL heads received

records.Description: A first-of-its-kind initiative in India designed to attract large-scale foreign capital into the state's manufacturing and services sectors. Eligible projects require a minimum capital investment of ₹100 crore and receive a 25–35% capital subsidy, along with direct land subsidy.

The Centre of Excellence (CoE) scheme under Uttar Pradesh's Industrial Investment & Employment Promotion Policy 2022 (IIEPP-2022) is designed to foster specialised R&D and innovation hubs across strategic and sunrise sectors in the state. Eligible CoEs — covering private companies, PSUs, and government-backed entities — receive a financial grant-in-aid of up to ₹10 crore over five years from the date of establishment.
records.Pending deliverables: 1. UPInvest Technical committee to evaluate application

records.Scheme: Gujarat Aatmanirbhar Scheme for Assistance to Large Industries and Thrust Sector
records.Description: The scheme covers manufacturing units with fixed capital investment exceeding ₹50 crore — for both new establishments and expansions — across general and thrust sector categories. Key incentives include an interest subsidy of up to 7% for 6–10 years depending on sector and taluka category, EPF reimbursement at 12% of basic salary for 10 years, and electricity duty exemption.

records.Scheme: Gujarat Industrial Policy 2020: Scheme for assistance for Research & Development Activities
records.Description:
The scheme is structured across four sub-components: assistance to establish R&D/product development centres, support for contract or sponsored research work, assistance to State/Central Government R&D institutions and laboratories, and assistance to industrial associations for setting up R&D laboratories.
Private companies and institutions can receive support of up to ₹5 crore for setting up R&D and product development centres

records.Scheme: Electronics Component Manufacturing Scheme 2025 - CAM Project
records.Description: Notified in April 2025 with a ₹40,000 crore budget outlay over six years, ECMS offers turnover-linked, capex-linked and hybrid incentives to boost domestic electronics component production. Incentives are disbursed across sub-assemblies, bare components, selected bare components, and supply-chain/capital equipment categories to attract investment and reduce import dependence.
